name: Meter
description: A visual indicator showing a scalar value within a known range. Built on Base UI Meter with StyleX styling.

Root.description: The container that provides meter state (value, min, max) to child parts.

value.description: The current value of the meter.

format.description: Formatting options for the displayed value.

Label.description: An accessible label for the meter.

Track.description: The background track of the meter.

size.description: Height of the track (sm=4px, md=8px, lg=12px).

Indicator.description: The filled portion of the meter, proportional to the value.

Value.description: Displays the formatted value of the meter.

intents.reasoning: Meter displays a scalar measurement within a known range. Use for disk usage, battery level, password strength, etc.
Meter with destructive color communicates when a value has crossed a critical threshold.

avoidWhen.scenario: Showing progress of a task that will complete
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Progress for indeterminate or determinate task progress. Meter is for static scalar values, not ongoing operations.
Use Progress with indeterminate mode or a spinner. Meter represents a measurement, not a loading state.
Meter shows a continuous bar. For discrete steps, use a rating component or radio group.

examples.name: colors
examples.code:
<>
  <Meter.Root value={30}>
    <Meter.Track>
      <Meter.Indicator color="default" />
    </Meter.Track>
  </Meter.Root>
  <Meter.Root value={60}>
    <Meter.Track>
      <Meter.Indicator color="secondary" />
    </Meter.Track>
  </Meter.Root>
  <Meter.Root value={90}>
    <Meter.Track>
      <Meter.Indicator color="destructive" />
    </Meter.Track>
  </Meter.Root>
</>

examples.name: sizes
examples.code:
<>
  <Meter.Root value={50}>
    <Meter.Track size="sm">
      <Meter.Indicator />
    </Meter.Track>
  </Meter.Root>
  <Meter.Root value={50}>
    <Meter.Track size="md">
      <Meter.Indicator />
    </Meter.Track>
  </Meter.Root>
  <Meter.Root value={50}>
    <Meter.Track size="lg">
      <Meter.Indicator />
    </Meter.Track>
  </Meter.Root>
</>
